Document:
## Mutinies and riots require admin permission.
[bold]A mutiny is only valid when you are assigned the mutineer role by a muntineer leader, or are assigned as a leader by an admin. This will give the character a unique skull-helmet icon visible to everyone, and a message in the chat. Anyone without this icon may not participate as a mutineer.[/bold]
### General
- Do not goad other players into a mutiny before it is approved.\n(For example, asking others if they want to mutiny, before becoming a mutineer.) - All players, including hostile forces, must obtain admin permission before engaging in riots, mutinies or similar activity. - Strikes, revolutions, riots, cults, prison riots (excluding hostile forces), and any other type of similar behavior are strictly forbidden without permission. - Staff reserve the right to grant or deny these, and to decide if the reason given is sufficient. - Mutinies are not always indicative of a bad commander, and shouldn't be taken personally. Have fun with it on the receiving end and be open to advice from others! Do not target the player non-constructively in OOC channels.
### Mutiny Restrictions
- Mutiny cannot occur during hijack, and any ongoing mutiny and hostilities will, and must, be ended upon hijack. - Only the current Commander can be mutinied against. They must be given a chance to stand down peacefully. - Mutineers may escalate to physical interactions, such as shoving MPs out of the way or blowing open walls with explosives, and may further escalate to lethal weapons if they are engaged by the MPs who are using any crowd control tools or attempting to make arrests on mutineers. - During the mutiny, attacking any uninvolved, medical, or surrendered personnel is forbidden, as is permanently killing anyone involved in the combat, if there is any. - If you have surrendered during a mutiny, you must remain as such. - You may not change sides or pretend to be non-hostile before engaging.
### Mutiny Outcomes - If the mutiny fails to depose the commander, all sides must be given medical treatment. The Commander is permitted to deny treatment and revival, or battlefield execute mutineers if they are capable. Remaining mutineers will be charged per marine law. See marine law for full information. - If the mutiny succeeds, all sides must be given medical treatment. The next in the commander succession line should be put in charge.
### Steps to Starting a Mutiny - Avoid goading other players into mutiny until you have confirmation to start. - Request a mutiny in ahelp with a valid mutiny reason. - After becoming a mutiny leader, you will gain the ability to invite others through an action added to the action bar. (Non-leader mutineers will not be invited through ahelp.) - The mutiny leader must have 3 invited members minimum to proceed.
[bold]Examples of mutiny reasons:[/bold] - Commander incompetence, where the next in line would lead better. - Commander making a decision that results in sudden mass marine casualties.\n(Note that bombardments are not always the fault of CIC.) - Commander takes no actions in leading. - Commander refuses to allow planet-side evacuation.
[bold]Examples of invalid mutiny reasons:[/bold] - Not liking the commander. - Wanting to kill the commander. - Trying to take revenge against the commander or CIC. - Having no intent to redeploy if the mutiny succeeds.
### Evacuation Mutinies
- These can be done at any point a shipside evacuation (like during hijack) is allowed, but the Commander is refusing evacuation requests. - They may be done by any number of people without permission, with the intent to evacuate from the warship and save their own lives and the lives of others that wish to evacuate. - The Commander must still be given a chance to allow the evacuation through negotiation. - The most minimal amount of force should be used to start the evacuation. - Any roles may join and aid this mutiny, even those normally prevented.
